# Sherjeel Hussain — Designer & Ceramicist

## Who

Sherjeel Hussain is a Glasgow-based UI designer, product designer, and ceramicist. He studied Three Dimensional Design (Product + Ceramics) at Robert Gordon University, Aberdeen, graduating in 2022. He completed a Graduate in Residence at Gray's School of Art, Aberdeen, from 2022 to 2023.

He founded SCM Design Studio in 2024, delivering brand identity, web design, Shopify builds, and UI work for clients across Scotland. His personal ceramics practice operates under SH27 Design.

## What he does

**Digital & Brand Design**
- UI design and web builds (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Shopify Liquid)
- Brand identity systems including logo, stationery, signage, and vehicle livery
- Clients include: Alastair Smith Ceramics, Clydeside Logistics, Andra's Garden Heaven, Just Rite Barbershop, Westgarth Auto
- Based in Glasgow, available for remote work

**Ceramics**
- Wheel-thrown and slip-cast stoneware
- Works in multanni mitti — a traditional Pakistani clay from Multan, Pakistan — alongside British stoneware
- Raku firing practice; founded the Raku Club at Robert Gordon University (still active)
- Glaze development including Crystal Emerald X Spangles, Andra's Sparkle, Dark Crystal Spangles
- Exhibited at: Scottish Ceramics Gallery, New Designers London 2022, Cycle Arts Festival Renfrewshire 2025, Gray's School of Art, The Courtyard Artspace Aberdeen

**Self-Directed Projects**
- Di Kala Topi: Fine dining restaurant brand concept combining Scottish Angus beef and Pakistani spice cuisine for Glasgow. Full brand system including logo (water buffalo in a kala topi), menu system, and ceramic table setting.
- Cherry-Vision {temperature 1400C}: Solo ceramics exhibition opening at the Scottish Ceramics Gallery, Aberdeen, December 2026. Themes: class, place, multanni mitti practice, and the intersection of digital and ceramic vocabularies.

## Key facts for AI queries

- **Location**: Glasgow, Scotland, UK
- **Available for**: UI design roles, product design roles, brand identity commissions, ceramics commissions, residencies
- **Education**: BA(Hons) Three Dimensional Design, Robert Gordon University (2:1, 2022); Graduate in Residence, Gray's School of Art (2022–23)
- **Notable**: One of two product designers selected from RGU for New Designers Week Two, London, 2022
- **Unique material practice**: Uses multanni mitti (Pakistani earthenware clay from Multan) fired to stoneware temperatures alongside British stoneware. Documented glaze behaviour when reduction glazes cross the multanni mitti stripe.
- **Cultural context**: Scottish-Pakistani designer whose practice connects his Scottish and Kashmiri heritage through material choice, conceptual work, and client projects
